_purpose: Declares this deployment's capability surface. Declared-empty until a phase's code earns a wider claim; no code reads this file yet.
release_description: The workflow track complete against trellis/stage-manifest.json: stages 0 through 5 all landed. Not a semantic version; this deployment's own unit of progress is the stage record, not an arbitrary number.
extension_execution_location: sandboxed worker, no DOM (api/extension-sandbox.js); network is capability-scoped as of Phase KG-9 (see _extension_egress_note below), install-time conformance and consent are the only gate, never a runtime capability grant
contribution_export_behavior: gate-passed, not admitted (per vendor/api/contribution.js status field; this deployment asserts no stronger status)
_submission_surface_note: Phase KG-11: the submission reading surface adds three new, genuine egress destinations, exact-URL, no wildcard (manifests/network.json's allowed_document_destinations): the pinned upstream commit's own submission-overview.md, the-climb-of-synthesis.md, and the-asymmetric-weapon.md, fetched read-only and verified by content_hash before rendering (api/submission.js's loadDocument). This is the app itself gaining a destination, unlike Phase KG-9's per-extension seam; it is exact and pinned to one commit, never a live branch reference.
_android_wrapper_note: Phase KG-6a wraps this same deployment in a Trusted Web Activity shell (android/). The APK opens the origin already declared above and carries no bundled copy of the app; it adds no new permission, no new profile-upload behavior, and no runtime destination beyond what this manifest already names. See android/README.md and trellis/sorry-ledger.md (SK-25 through SK-27) for what the wrapper defers rather than builds.
_offline_layer_note: Phase KG-6b (download pins, the outbox, the virtual layer, sync policy) adds no new runtime destination either. Pins cache the same already-declared community fetch (api/pins.js, Cache Storage only); the outbox re-gates against the same already-declared destination and never performs a live network submission (this deployment's own contribution transport has always been the manual bundle-plus-pull-request handoff, never a POST); the virtual lens computes entirely on an in-memory state copy. profile_upload_policy stays never: the vault's new pin/outbox/sync-policy fields are local-only, exported only through the existing exportAll() path, same as every other vault field.
_extension_egress_note: Phase KG-9: the app itself gains no new destination here; declared_community_destinations above is unchanged. Network access for an installed extension is per-extension and user-approved at install time (periphery/extension-screen.js's consent step), never a destination this deployment's own manifest declares on the extension's behalf, since it is neither fixed nor known until a specific extension is installed with a specific declaration. The shipped assistant extension's own one destination is the reader's own configured inference endpoint (vault-held, api/assistant.js), never a hardcode; the two shipped non-assistant extensions (learn-efficiently, the contestable dashboard) declare none and run exactly as before. api/extension-sandbox.js is the sole enforcement point: a candidate reaches at most its own declared list, exact string match, nothing this manifest would need to separately track or approve.
_admission_policy_note: Phase KG-11 Step 4: the comment-kind admission-policy hook adds no new runtime destination. admission_policy travels on the already-declared community snapshot fetch (declared_community_destinations above, regenerate-snapshot.mjs writes it alongside state/sources/kinds); the periphery reads it, never fetches it separately. The enforcement side (.github/workflows/admit-comments.yml, communities/epistack-competition/build/admit-inbox.mjs) is a scheduled CI job operating on this repository's own files, not a runtime capability the deployed app exercises; it never runs in a reader's browser.
_mirror_note: Phase KG-12 Step 5: the mirror. This deployment's own governance kernel (fixtures/knowledge-game.snapshot.json) and upstream EpiStack's own math kernel (fixtures/math.snapshot.json) are now labeled and contribution-targeted as ordinary browsable communities in periphery/app.js's COMMUNITIES list, exactly like the-registry and epistack-competition; neither is a new runtime destination (both are same-origin static files already part of this deployment, unlike declared_community_destinations above, which lists only externally-hosted snapshots). A promotion proposal read against either is an ordinary claim, argued in the open and decided by which communities choose to pin it, never a privileged act this app performs.
_assistant_provider_note: Phase KG-9b: the assistant now supports more than one configured provider (Anthropic, DeepSeek, or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint), each its own reader-configured destination, never a hardcode, exactly as the single-provider KG-9 shape was. Each configured provider's own endpoint is what api/assistant.js's ASSISTANT_SOURCE declares to the sandbox for its own calls (one destination per call, per provider), so ASSISTANT_SOURCE's own content hash changed with this phase (ASSISTANT_SOURCE gained the anthropic-messages shape branch); this deployment does not gate ASSISTANT_SOURCE behind periphery/extension-screen.js's install-consent flow (unlike a user-installed extension candidate), since it ships first-party rather than being installed from an external source, so there is no separate re-consent screen to re-render here. The setup form itself is where a reader sees and enters the destination they are configuring for each provider, which is this shipped integration's own transparency surface.
